title: conium maculatum subsp. divaricatum
intro: Conium maculatum subsp. divaricatum, also known as the poisonous hemlock, is a subspecies of the Conium maculatum plant, a flowering plant in the carrot family native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to relax muscles and relieve spasms. The health benefits of Conium maculatum subsp. divaricatum include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as acting as a sedative and anxiolytic. Conium maculatum subsp. divaricatum is often prepared as an herbal infusion or tincture, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
